relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks repair for hidden misalignment.
A soul may err without intending harm.
When priestly misalignment affects the people, repair must begin at the holy center.
The hand rests on the offering before YHWH.
The priest carries blood/life into the tent.
Blood/life is sprinkled before the veil.
The altar is marked and life poured at its base.
The inner fat is lifted.
The inward portions are removed.
They rise on the altar like peace-offering fat.
The rest of the bull is carried outward.
What bears misalignment is burned outside the camp.
A whole congregation can err while the matter is hidden.
When the hidden wrong becomes known, the assembly brings repair.
Elders lay hands on behalf of the people.
Blood/life is brought inward.
It is sprinkled before YHWH.
The altar is marked and the base receives blood/life.
The fat is lifted and offered.
Atonement/covering is made, and forgiveness is given.
The assembly’s offering is carried outside and burned.
A leader may also err and become guilty.
When the leader’s misalignment is made known, he brings a goat.
The hand rests on the offering in the holy place of slaughter.
The altar is marked with blood/life.
The priest makes covering, and the leader is forgiven.
A common person may also err and become guilty.
When the sin becomes known, the person brings a female goat.
The hand rests on the repair-offering.
Blood/life marks the altar.
The fat rises, covering is made, and forgiveness is given.
A lamb may also be brought for repair.
The hand rests on the lamb.
Blood/life marks the altar and base.
The offering rises, covering is made, and the person is forgiven.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 4 gives a path when hidden harm becomes visible: priest, community, leader, and common person can all return through repair and forgiveness.
